title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 209 — Astronauts for America
summary: The latest episode of 'This Week In Space' podcast features a discussion with Garrett Reisman and Steve Lindsey, leaders of the new organization Astronauts for America. The conversation focuses on their mission and insights within the space industry.

title: NASA's twin Voyager spacecraft are very low on power after nearly 50 years. How long can they keep going?
summary: NASA's Voyager spacecraft, launched in 1977, are running low on power after nearly 50 years in interstellar space. It remains uncertain how much longer they can continue operating.

title: NASA's Artemis 2 commander and astrophotographer team up to capture breathtaking, never-before-seen shots of the moon's far side
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 commander Reid Wiseman and astrophotographer Andrew McCarthy will collaborate to capture unprecedented images of the moon's far side. This partnership aims to produce breathtaking, never-before-seen views of the lunar surface.

title: Pentagon unveils trove of declassfied 'UFO' videos. How to see them all, from 'a football-shaped body' to 'a misshapen and uneven ball of white light'
summary: The Pentagon released a new set of declassified UFO videos, including nearly 30 clips featuring objects like a football-shaped body and a misshapen ball of white light. The documents were unveiled on May 8 as part of ongoing transparency efforts.

title: Black holes slamming into scorching stars may be causing mysterious blue flashes in the cosmos
summary: Astronomers suggest that Luminous Fast Blue Optical Transients (LFBOTs), bright blue cosmic explosions, may result from black holes or neutron stars colliding with the hottest type of stars. This discovery could explain the origin of these mysterious and energetic events.

title: Monopoly sucks, but this new 'Star Wars' video game spinoff might add some galactic sizzle
summary: A new 'Star Wars' video game spinoff is generating excitement in the gaming community. The article humorously references the game's potential to add 'galactic sizzle' despite the monopolistic nature of the industry.

title: How you'd really die in space: What sci-fi gets right (and wrong) about extra-terrestrial expirations
summary: The article discusses the inaccuracies in sci-fi portrayals of death in space, highlighting what scenarios are scientifically plausible versus exaggerated. It explores the real dangers of space exploration and how they differ from fictional depictions.

title: This is your best chance to spot the Milky Way's core in May
summary: In May, there is an opportunity to observe the Milky Way's core at its peak visibility in the spring sky. This event highlights the best chance to spot the glowing arch of our galaxy's center.

title: Blue Origin tests 1st moon lander ahead of lunar launch later this year (photo)
summary: Blue Origin is testing its first lunar lander at NASA centers ahead of a planned lunar mission later this year. The spacecraft is undergoing various tests to ensure readiness for the upcoming launch.

title: NASA’s SpaceX 34th Commercial Resupply Mission Overview
summary: NASA and SpaceX are targeting a mid-May launch for the 34th Commercial Resupply Mission to the International Space Station, carrying 6,500 pounds of supplies aboard a Dragon spacecraft on a Falcon 9 rocket.

title: NASA’s Psyche Mission Captures Mars During Gravity Assist Approach
summary: NASA's Psyche mission captured a colorized image of Mars during a gravity assist approach on May 3, 2026. The spacecraft will use the gravity assist on May 15 to adjust its trajectory toward asteroid Psyche.

title: I Am Artemis: Anton Kiriwas
summary: Anton Kiriwas, a senior technical integration manager for NASA's Exploration Ground Systems Program, shares his journey and inspiration from a job fair image of the Moon and Mars. The excerpt highlights his passion for space exploration and career path at NASA.

title: NASA, Industry Advance High Performance Spaceflight Computing
summary: NASA has been advancing high-performance spaceflight computing for decades, starting with the Apollo Guidance Computers in the 1960s. The agency continues to develop radiation-hardened processors as the foundation for mission success.

title: Glowing Views from the Space Station
summary: NASA astronaut Chris Williams captured a stunning image of the Milky Way rising above Earth's atmospheric glow, known as airglow, from a SpaceX Dragon spacecraft docked to the International Space Station on April 13, 2026. The phenomenon occurs when atoms and molecules in the upper atmosphere emit light after being excited by sunlight.

title: NASA Names Brian Hughes to Launch Operations Role
summary: NASA appointed Brian Hughes as senior director of launch operations at Kennedy Space Center. He will oversee NASA's launch infrastructure and report to NASA Headquarters.

title: NASA Fuel Cell Tests Pave Way for Energy Storage on Moon
summary: NASA conducted fuel cell tests to advance energy storage solutions for future lunar missions. The tests involved a cylindrical fuel cell system with extensive sensor instrumentation.

title: Tracy Arm’s Post-Tsunami Landscape
summary: A landslide-triggered tsunami in summer 2025 stripped vegetation from the shore of Tracy Arm, a glacial fjord. The event highlights the environmental impact of natural disasters in glacial regions.

title: Meet the Fleet: NASA Armstrong Continues Legacy of Flight Research
summary: NASA's Armstrong Flight Research Center has expanded its fleet with new aircraft, including two F-15 jets and a Pilatus PC-12 plane, to support ongoing flight research and science missions. The center continues the legacy of pioneers like Neil Armstrong in experimental aviation.

title: NASA Welcomes Paraguay as 67th Artemis Accords Signatory
summary: Paraguay became the 67th signatory to the Artemis Accords during a ceremony in Asunción, committing to principles for civil space exploration. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman welcomed Paraguay's participation in the international coalition.

title: Full Stack Flight 12 prepares for WDR
summary: Full Stack Flight 12 is preparing for a Wet Dress Rehearsal (WDR) after Booster 19 successfully completed a full-duration, full-thrust static fire test with 33 engines on May 7, 2026.

title: China prepares cargo, crew, and deep space missions, as commercial sector steps towards reusability
summary: China is preparing to launch cargo and crew missions to its Tiangong space station while advancing its commercial sector towards reusability. The country is also working on deep space missions.

title: Pad 1 conversion ongoing, with Flight 12 nearing
summary: Pad 1 is undergoing conversion after the end of Flight 11, with Flight 12 approaching. The old design of Pad 1 is being updated for future missions.

title: Launch Preview: three launches — a calm before the storm?
summary: Three launches are scheduled, including two SpaceX Falcon 9 missions and one Chang Zheng 7 mission. This may precede a busier period in spaceflight activity.

title: SpaceX launches CAS500-2 and 45 other payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit
summary: SpaceX launched the CAS500-2 satellite and 45 other payloads to a Sun-synchronous orbit aboard a Falcon 9 rocket as part of its third rideshare mission in 2026. This marks another successful deployment for SpaceX's rideshare program, expanding access to space for multiple payloads.

title: SpaceX aims for mid-May Starship Flight 12 launch with revised trajectory
summary: SpaceX is targeting a mid-May launch for its Starship Flight 12 mission, featuring a revised trajectory. The upcoming flight aims to build on previous test objectives with improved performance.

title: Russia debuts new rocket with maiden Soyuz-5 launch
summary: Russia successfully launched its new Soyuz-5 rocket for the first time on Saturday, marking a significant milestone in its space program. The maiden flight demonstrates the country's efforts to modernize its launch capabilities.

title: Next NASA ISS astronaut, doctor discusses upcoming mission
summary: A NASA astronaut and doctor who has experience in mass casualty events and overseas service is set to discuss their upcoming mission to the ISS. The astronaut will highlight their preparations and role in the mission.

title: SpaceX launches Falcon Heavy on Wednesday after 18 month hiatus
summary: SpaceX successfully launched its Falcon Heavy rocket after an 18-month hiatus. The mission marked a significant return for the heavy-lift vehicle.

title: Artemis III core stage assembly arrives at the Kennedy Space Center
summary: The core stage assembly for Artemis III has arrived at the Kennedy Space Center, following the historic Artemis II mission. This marks a key step toward NASA's planned crewed lunar landing.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry news item, though the specific details were not provided in the given text.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA released a booklet highlighting 50 hallmark achievements over its 50-year history. The publication celebrates the agency's significant milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) to enhance space exploration through training, operations, simulations, and public engagement. This initiative aims to modernize space missions and make them more accessible.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: The article discusses a critical test involving parachute baking, likely for a space mission. This process ensures the reliability and functionality of parachutes under extreme conditions.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: ESA is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination of Mars. The parachute, made of nylon and Kevlar, will slow the rover's descent during its 2028 landing to search for signs of life.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing objects in space. This innovation could significantly enhance satellite servicing and debris removal missions.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, paving the way for its planned launch in early 2027 to search for terrestrial planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual SME Forum 2026, held on June 8-9 at ESA Headquarters in Paris, with an online preparatory session in April-May. The event aims to gather feedback on topics affecting small and medium-sized enterprises in the space sector.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste's first navigation signal was successfully received, marking a significant milestone for the project. The exact location or context of this achievement was not specified in the provided news snippet.

title: Lunar Outpost Closes $30M Series B, Unveils New Rover
summary: Lunar Outpost raised $30 million in a Series B funding round and unveiled a new lunar rover. The investment reflects investor interest in NASA's plans for US lunar presence.

title: Rocket Lab To Acquire Motiv Space Systems
summary: Rocket Lab will acquire space robotics company Motiv Space Systems, with the acquisition expected to close in Q2 and Motiv to be rebranded as Rocket Lab Robotics. The deal was announced alongside Rocket Lab's Q1 earnings.

title: HawkEye 360 Raises $416M in IPO
summary: HawkEye 360, a Virginia-based company, raised $416 million in its IPO by pricing 16 million shares at $26 each. The company's S-1 filing on May 1 had initially set the price range at $24-$26.

title: SatVu Positions Thermal Imaging as the Missing EO Layer
summary: SatVu has resumed operations, emphasizing thermal imaging as a critical missing layer in Earth observation (EO). The company aims to enhance EO capabilities with its thermal imaging technology.

title: South Korea Pushes to Commercialize Quantum Research
summary: South Korea is accelerating efforts to commercialize quantum research, aiming to establish itself as a key player in the emerging quantum technology sector. The country is pushing forward with initiatives to transition quantum advancements from research labs to practical applications.

title: Overview Energy Wins Air Force Contract
summary: Overview Energy has secured an Air Force contract to provide continuous power to military bases using space-based solar energy, addressing vulnerabilities in fuel supply chains. The company aims to mitigate strategic threats by leveraging solar power day and night.

title: Astranis Raises $450M+, Including $300M Series E
summary: Astranis raised over $450 million in a funding round led by Snowpoint Ventures and Franklin Templeton, valuing the company at $2.8 billion. This Series E round highlights significant investor confidence in the space industry.

title: Scout Space Closes $18M Series A
summary: Scout Space has secured $18 million in Series A funding to expand its operations. Plans include building a new satellite sensor factory near Washington, D.C., and nearly doubling the company's workforce within 18 months.

title: LiveEO Raises €28M+ to Fund its First Constellation
summary: LiveEO, a German company, has raised over €28 million to fund its first satellite constellation. The funds will be used to scale its business, launch the constellation, and develop technology to meet the increasing demand for Earth observation (EO) data.

title: Commerce Aiming to Open Novel Space Applications This Summer
summary: The Office of Space Commerce plans to streamline licensing for novel space missions by this summer. This initiative aims to position the OSC as a central authority for such applications.

title: Greek Government Taps Planet for New Satellite Data Deal
summary: The Greek government has signed a 2-year, 7-figure contract with Planet for satellite data services. The deal highlights Planet's expansion in the European market.

title: Operational AI is Hitting the Limits of Earth Observation Data
summary: AI in Earth Observation is transitioning from experimental use on curated datasets to operational deployment, where models must perform consistently across diverse data sources. This shift highlights challenges in scaling AI applications due to limitations in available Earth observation data.

title: Globalstar Grows Revenue 17% in Q1 Amid Amazon Acquisition Process
summary: Globalstar reported a 17% year-over-year revenue growth in Q1 2026 amid its acquisition process by Amazon. The satellite operator's financial performance highlights its strategic position in the industry.

title: Rocket Lab Q1 Takeaways: Neutron Momentum, SBI Deal and Robotics Acquisition
summary: Rocket Lab reported record quarterly revenue in Q1 2026 and highlighted progress on its Neutron launch vehicle, a robotics acquisition, and an SBI deal.

title: Skyroot Secures $60M in Funding, Becoming India’s First Space ‘Unicorn’
summary: Skyroot Aerospace, an Indian launch startup, secured $60 million in funding, making it India's first space 'unicorn'. The company is a rising star in India's space economy.

title: HawkEye 360 Raises $416M, Pricing at High End for IPO
summary: HawkEye 360 raised $416 million in its IPO, becoming the second space company to go public this year. The signals intelligence company began trading after securing significant funding.

title: Spire Says New German Facility Can Produce Up to 100 Satellites Per Year
summary: Spire has opened a new satellite manufacturing facility in Munich with the capacity to produce up to 100 satellites per year. The facility is designed for end-to-end satellite production.

title: MDA Space Satellite Revenue Grows 40% in Q1, Greenley Says ‘No Change’ Due to Globalstar Deal
summary: MDA Space reported 32% year-over-year revenue growth in Q1 2026 and over 40% growth in its satellite manufacturing business, driven by contracts for Telesat Lightspeed. CEO Mike Greenley stated that the Globalstar deal did not impact these results.

title: Enabled by AI, NGA Director Focused on Mission, Transformation, and Workforce
summary: The National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency (NGA) is leveraging AI to enhance its mission, transformation, and workforce to address evolving threats and warfighter needs. The agency's new director is emphasizing resilience and the adoption of emerging technologies.

title: Telespazio to Distribute Greenerwave Multi-Orbit Terminals to European Defense Market
summary: Telespazio France will distribute multi-orbit terminals developed by Greenerwave to the European defense market under a new agreement. The collaboration aims to enhance satellite communication capabilities for defense applications.
